body:
## Summary

Formal AI's HTTP server should be a **universal, multi-protocol API gateway** in front of the one symbolic solver: it must speak *every* major agent-facing API dialect ‚Äî OpenAI (Chat Completions + Responses), Anthropic Messages, **Google Gemini (`generateContent` / `streamGenerateContent`)**, and **Vertex AI** ‚Äî so that any agent CLI or SDK can drive Formal AI unchanged. The reasoning core stays single and shared; only the request/response *envelope* differs per protocol.

To make room for this, the URL space must be **reorganized by protocol family**: every OpenAI-shaped route moves from `/v1/...` to **`/api/openai/v1/...`**, and each other protocol gets its own sibling prefix (`/api/gemini/...`, `/api/vertex/...`, `/api/anthropic/...`, ...). This gives us a clean namespace instead of overloading `/v1` for one vendor's shape.

This is the umbrella issue for that initiative. It builds on and subsumes the streaming gap found in #602.

## Motivation ‚Äî found by actually driving external CLIs

We drove real agent CLIs at the running server (`formal-ai serve`, v0.254.0, stable rustc 1.96.1):

| CLI | Reaches our server? | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Codex** (`codex-cli 0.142.0`) | Yes (Responses wire API) | **Fails** ‚Äî `/v1/responses` ignores `stream:true`, returns one JSON body and closes; Codex waits for `response.completed` SSE events, retries 5√ó, dies: `stream disconnected before completion`. (Tracked in #602.) Codex 0.142 also dropped `wire_api = "chat"`, so Responses+SSE is the only path. |
| **Gemini CLI** (`@google/gemini-cli 0.38.2`) | **No** | The official build has **no OpenAI-compatible provider** at all ‚Äî its `AuthType` enum is only `LOGIN_WITH_GOOGLE`, `USE_GEMINI`, `USE_VERTEX_AI`, `COMPUTE_ADC`, `GATEWAY`, `LEGACY_CLOUD_SHELL`. `OPENAI_BASE_URL` is ignored; it falls through to Google auth. The **only** way it can ever target us is if we expose a **native Gemini `generateContent` endpoint** (a different API shape from OpenAI's). |

So supporting the Gemini/Vertex ecosystem is not an OpenAI-compat tweak ‚Äî it requires implementing their native wire shapes. Hence this umbrella issue.

## Current route surface (`src/server.rs`)

Everything vendor-facing currently lives under `/v1`:

- `GET  /v1/models`
- `POST /v1/chat/completions`  ‚Äî honors `stream:true` (single-chunk SSE, see caveat below)
- `POST /v1/responses`         ‚Äî **does NOT** honor `stream:true` (always one JSON body) ‚Üê #602
- `POST /v1/messages`          ‚Äî Anthropic Messages (honors `stream:true`)
- `GET  /v1/graph`, `/v1/bundle`, `/v1/links`, `POST /v1/links/query`, `/v1/memory*` ‚Äî Formal-AI-native introspection

**Streaming caveat (affects #602 too):** `sse_response()` (`src/server.rs:259`) emits the *entire* answer as **one** `data: {json}\n\n` chunk followed by `data: [DONE]`. It is SSE-framed but not incremental, and ‚Äî critically ‚Äî for the Responses endpoint it would still emit a Chat-Completions-shaped blob, **not** the `response.created ‚Üí response.output_text.delta ‚Üí response.completed` event sequence Codex requires. So "add SSE to `/v1/responses`" means emitting the correct Responses event *protocol*, not just wrapping the existing JSON.

## Proposed design

### 1. Protocol-namespaced URL space

Introduce `/api/<protocol>/...` and move OpenAI routes under it:

```
/api/openai/v1/models
/api/openai/v1/chat/completions
/api/openai/v1/responses
/api/anthropic/v1/messages
/api/gemini/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent
/api/gemini/v1beta/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent
/api/vertex/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{loc}/publishers/google/models/{model}:generateContent
```

Formal-AI-native routes (`/graph`, `/bundle`, `/links`, `/memory`) move to a neutral prefix, e.g. `/api/formal-ai/...` (or stay at root ‚Äî decide during design).

**Backward compatibility:** keep the old `/v1/...` paths working as aliases (301/308 redirect or internal re-dispatch) for at least one release, with a deprecation note, so existing clients pointed at `‚Ä¶:8080/v1` don't break.

### 2. One solver, many envelopes (adapter pattern)

Each protocol is a thin **codec** that (a) parses its request shape into the shared internal request, (b) calls the existing `UniversalSolver`, and (c) renders the internal answer into that protocol's response shape ‚Äî both non-streaming JSON and that protocol's streaming event format. The Anthropic path (`src/anthropic.rs`) already demonstrates this seam; Gemini and Vertex follow the same structure.

### 3. Correct streaming per protocol

- OpenAI Chat: `chat.completion.chunk` events + `data: [DONE]`.
- OpenAI Responses: `response.created` ‚Üí `response.output_item.added` ‚Üí `response.output_text.delta`‚Ä¶ ‚Üí `response.output_item.done` ‚Üí `response.completed`. **(This is the #602 fix.)**
- Anthropic: `message_start` ‚Üí `content_block_delta`‚Ä¶ ‚Üí `message_stop`.
- Gemini: `streamGenerateContent` chunked `GenerateContentResponse` objects.

### 4. Per-protocol model listing

Return the model-list shape each client expects (OpenAI `{"data":[...]}`, Gemini `{"models":[...]}` under `/api/gemini/v1beta/models`, etc.). This also clears the Codex `failed to refresh available models` warning noted in #602.

## Scope / sub-issues

This umbrella should be split into shippable, independently testable slices (each with an end-to-end test driving the real HTTP transport with a real client, so regressions are caught in CI, not only by the in-process in-repo driver):

- [ ] **S1 ‚Äî URL reorg**: move OpenAI routes to `/api/openai/v1/*`, add `/api/...` namespace, keep `/v1/*` aliases with deprecation. Tests: old + new paths both resolve.
- [ ] **S2 ‚Äî OpenAI Responses SSE** (subsumes #602): correct `response.*` event stream; `codex exec "hi"` completes end-to-end.
- [ ] **S3 ‚Äî OpenAI Chat SSE correctness**: real `chat.completion.chunk` events (incremental if feasible).
- [ ] **S4 ‚Äî Gemini native**: `:generateContent` + `:streamGenerateContent` + `/v1beta/models`; an OpenAI-compat *or* native Gemini client completes `hi`.
- [ ] **S5 ‚Äî Vertex AI native**: Vertex publisher-model routes mapping onto the same solver.
- [ ] **S6 ‚Äî Per-protocol model listing** and error-shape parity.
- [ ] **S7 ‚Äî Docs**: `ARCHITECTURE.md` + `README` document every supported protocol, its base URL, and a copy-paste client config (codex, claude, gemini/vertex SDKs).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The server exposes OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Vertex request/response shapes, each under its own `/api/<protocol>/...` prefix, all backed by the single `UniversalSolver`.
- [ ] `http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/openai/v1` is the OpenAI base URL; `‚Ä¶/v1` still works as a deprecated alias.
- [ ] Streaming works correctly for every protocol that defines a streaming format (verified against a real client per protocol).
- [ ] `codex exec "hi"` completes end-to-end (closes #602).
- [ ] A Gemini/Vertex-shaped client (SDK or compatible CLI) completes `hi` against the native endpoint.
- [ ] Per-protocol model-listing shapes are correct; no client emits a model-metadata error.
- [ ] End-to-end transport tests exist in CI for each protocol.

## Environment

- `formal-ai 0.254.0`, `serve` on `127.0.0.1:8080`, stable rustc 1.96.1, macOS aarch64
- `codex-cli 0.142.0`, `@google/gemini-cli 0.38.2`

## Related

- #602 ‚Äî OpenAI Responses SSE streaming (becomes sub-issue S2 here)

---

## Documentation requirement (applies to this issue)

This issue is not complete until it ships **clear, copy-pasteable, end-to-end-verified documentation** (in `README.md` / `ARCHITECTURE.md` and/or `docs/`) for how a user configures and uses the relevant CLI/tooling against Formal AI ‚Äî such that following the doc produces a working result (e.g. a `hi` reply, or the documented command succeeding) **without reverse-engineering the wire format or config**. Every accepted client/model/config surface introduced here must be documented with a concrete, tested example.

comments.body:
## Correction + verified config details for Codex and Gemini

I did a full documentation pass on both CLIs (installed versions **codex 0.142.5**, **gemini-cli 0.49.0**) and need to **correct** one claim in the issue body: the official Gemini CLI **can** be pointed at a custom base URL ‚Äî I was wrong to say it can't. This actually *strengthens* the case for native Gemini support here.

### Codex ‚Äî no config workaround; streaming is mandatory
From the official config reference (`developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference`), `model_providers.<id>` supports: `base_url`, `env_key`, `env_http_headers`, `http_headers`, `query_params`, `request_max_retries` (4), `stream_max_retries` (5), `stream_idle_timeout_ms` (300000), `wire_api`.
- **`wire_api`: `"responses"` is the only supported value** ‚Äî `chat` is removed. (Matches the runtime error we saw.)
- **There is no option to disable streaming / force non-streaming**, and no model-listing/metadata key.
- **Conclusion:** Codex *always* streams over the Responses API. The only fix is server-side Responses SSE ‚Äî tracked as **#602 / sub-issue S2**. Re-tested `codex exec "hi"` on 0.142.5: fails identically (`stream disconnected before completion`).

### Gemini ‚Äî base-URL override DOES exist (correction)
The gemini-cli 0.49.0 config reference documents:
- **`GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL`** ‚Äî *"Overrides the default base URL for Gemini API requests (when using `gemini-api-key` authentication)."* Explicitly permits `http://localhost` / `127.0.0.1` (HTTPS requirement waived for localhost).
- **`GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL`** ‚Äî same, for `vertex-ai` auth.
- **`CODE_ASSIST_ENDPOINT`** ‚Äî overrides the OAuth code-assist endpoint.

**Key point:** `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL` changes only *where* the request is sent, **not the wire protocol** ‚Äî gemini-cli still sends its native `generateContent` / `streamGenerateContent` shape (not OpenAI). So the official CLI **can** be aimed at our server via:

```sh
export GEMINI_API_KEY="anything"                       # selects gemini-api-key auth
export GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/gemini"
gemini -m gemini-... -p "hi"
```

‚Ä¶and "hi" will work **as soon as our server answers the native Gemini `generateContent` protocol (+ its SSE) at that base URL** ‚Äî i.e. **sub-issue S4**. My earlier `IneligibleTierError` was caused by leaving it on the default Google-OAuth path (which ignores the base URL); the correct path is `gemini-api-key` auth **plus** `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL`.

**Net effect on this issue:** native Gemini support (S4) is even more valuable than first stated ‚Äî it makes the *real, official Gemini CLI* work against Formal AI, not just a Gemini SDK. Same for Vertex via `GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL` (S5). This aligns cleanly with the proposed `/api/gemini/...` and `/api/vertex/...` prefixes.

## Added ask: clear per-protocol / per-CLI configuration documentation

This umbrella should also deliver **clear, copy-pasteable documentation** for configuring each supported client CLI/SDK against Formal AI, verified to print a `hi` reply. At minimum:

- **Codex** ‚Äî `model_providers` block, `wire_api = "responses"`, `codex exec "hi"`. (see #602)
- **opencode** ‚Äî `opencode.json` custom `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` provider, `opencode run -m <provider>/<model> "hi"`. (see #604)
- **Gemini CLI** ‚Äî `GEMINI_API_KEY=<any>` + `GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/gemini` (`gemini-api-key` auth), `gemini -p "hi"`. (sub-issue S4)
- **Vertex** ‚Äî `GOOGLE_VERTEX_BASE_URL` + `vertex-ai` auth. (sub-issue S5)
- Base URLs per protocol prefix (`/api/openai/v1`, `/api/gemini/...`, `/api/vertex/...`, `/api/anthropic/...`).

**Acceptance addition (S7):** each documented client config is verified end-to-end (`hi` returns a reply) and the doc requires no wire-format reverse-engineering.
Blocks #606 (the `with-formal-ai` wrapper CLI, which can only complete a turn once this is resolved).
Related sub-work: #607 (bash/shell tool_calls) and #608 (standard reasoning/thinking fields + streaming) ‚Äî both feed the multi-protocol client-compatibility goal.

title: Universal multi-protocol API server: support OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Vertex shapes under /api/<protocol>/ (move OpenAI routes to /api/openai/v1)
